lesson: When writing Supabase queries in TypeScript, use .throwOnError() instead of manual error checks. Because the generated types assume success and manual checks are redundant noise.
When a Next.js dev server seems stale, check for zombie processes on port 3000 before restarting. Because Next.js processes outlive their parent terminal session.
When an agent hits a flaky test, grep the test name in ~/.skill-memory/lessons.jsonl before retrying. Because the same flake has probably been logged before.
